annotations.label: Bayes' theorem (statement)

anchor.snippet: Bayes' theorem is stated mathematically as the following equation

annotations.note: `cond_eq_inv_mul_cond_mul` states μ[t|s] = (μ s)⁻¹ * μ[s|t] * μ t, exactly P(A|B)=P(B|A)P(A)/P(B).

annotations.label: Posterior / conditional probability P(A|B)

anchor.snippet: It is also called the posterior probability of

annotations.note: `cond` defines the conditional probability measure and `cond_apply` gives μ[t|s] = (μ s)⁻¹ * μ (s ∩ t).

annotations.label: Likelihood P(B|A)

anchor.snippet: It can also be interpreted as the likelihood function evaluated at

annotations.note: For events the likelihood P(B|A) is just the conditional probability `cond`, though the likelihood-function concept is not separately named.

annotations.label: Prior / marginal probability

anchor.snippet: often called 'the prior probability' (prior to new evidence)

annotations.note: Prior/marginal probability is just measure application μ s with no dedicated 'prior' definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Derivation from joint and conditional probabilities

anchor.snippet: Bayes' theorem may be derived from the relation between joint and conditional probabilities

annotations.note: `cond_mul_eq_inter` states μ[t|s] * μ s = μ (s ∩ t), the joint–conditional relation underlying the derivation.

anchors.snippet: Bayes' theorem may be derived from the relation between joint and conditional probabilities

annotations.label: Extended form via law of total probability

anchor.snippet: are mutually exclusive and exhaustive, i.e., one of them is certain to occur but no two can occur together

annotations.note: The law of total probability over a finite partition is formalized, but the combined extended Bayes formula is not a single declaration.

anchors.snippet: are mutually exclusive and exhaustive, i.e., one of them is certain to occur but no two can occur together

annotations.label: Two-term form for A and not-A

anchor.snippet: are a mutually exclusive and exhaustive pair

annotations.note: `cond_add_cond_compl_eq` formalizes the two-term total-probability denominator, but the combined two-term Bayes formula is not stated directly.

anchors.snippet: are a mutually exclusive and exhaustive pair

annotations.label: Prior probability (degree of belief)

anchor.snippet: is the prior probability , the initial degree of belief in A

annotations.note: The 'degree of belief' interpretation of P(A) is just measure application; no Bayesian-prior definition exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Prior belief that A is false

anchor.snippet: is the corresponding initial degree of belief that A is false

annotations.note: P(¬A) is plain measure of the complement μ sᶜ; no dedicated concept formalized.

annotations.label: Likelihood given A true

anchor.snippet: the degree of belief in B given that A is true

annotations.note: P(B|A) is the conditional probability measure `cond`.

annotations.label: Likelihood given A false

anchor.snippet: the degree of belief in B given that A is false

annotations.note: P(B|¬A) is `cond` conditioned on the complement set.

annotations.label: Posterior probability (degree of belief)

anchor.snippet: is the posterior probability , the probability of A after taking into account B

annotations.note: The posterior P(A|B) is the conditional probability `cond` (with a dedicated `posterior` kernel for the general case).

annotations.label: Frequentist interpretation

anchor.snippet: the frequentist interpretations , probability measures a

annotations.note: A philosophical interpretation of probability, not a mathematical statement to formalize.

annotations.label: Bayes' theorem for events A and B

anchor.snippet: For events A and B , provided that

annotations.note: `cond_eq_inv_mul_cond_mul` is Bayes' theorem for two events.

annotations.label: Posterior proportional to prior times likelihood

anchor.snippet: the posterior is proportional to the prior times the likelihood

annotations.note: `posterior_eq_withDensity` expresses the posterior as the prior weighted by the likelihood (rnDeriv), the precise form of posterior ∝ prior × likelihood.

annotations.label: Bayes' theorem for continuous random variables

anchor.snippet: For two continuous random variables X and Y , Bayes' theorem may be analogously derived

annotations.note: The kernel posterior density formula generalizes the continuous-variable Bayes density quotient f(x|y)=f(y|x)f(x)/f(y).

annotations.label: Bayes' theorem (general measure-theoretic case)

anchor.snippet: be the conditional distribution of

annotations.note: The `posterior` kernel and `posterior_eq_withDensity` give the conditional distribution and its Radon–Nikodym density, the measure-theoretic Bayes' theorem.

annotations.label: Existence/uniqueness via Radon–Nikodym

anchor.snippet: Existence and uniqueness of the needed conditional expectation is a consequence of the Radon–Nikodym theorem

annotations.note: The Radon–Nikodym theorem is formalized, and conditional-distribution existence/uniqueness follows via disintegration (`eq_condKernel_of_measure_eq_compProd`).

annotations.label: Odds on a proposition

anchor.snippet: is called the odds on

annotations.note: Odds O(A)=P(A)/P(¬A) are not defined as a concept in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bayes' theorem in odds form

anchor.snippet: Bayes' theorem can be written using odds as follows

annotations.note: No odds notion exists in Mathlib, so the odds-form Bayes equation is not formalized.

annotations.label: Bayes factor / likelihood ratio

anchor.snippet: a quantity called the Bayes factor or likelihood ratio

annotations.note: Mathlib has the (log-)likelihood ratio of measures via `llr`/`rnDeriv`, but the event-based Bayes factor P(B|A)/P(B|¬A) is not a named declaration.

anchors.snippet: a quantity called the Bayes factor or likelihood ratio

annotations.label: Posterior odds = prior odds times likelihood

anchor.snippet: the posterior odds are the prior odds times the likelihood

annotations.note: Depends on the unformalized odds concept, so the odds-update relation is not formalized.

anchors.snippet: the posterior odds are the prior odds times the likelihood

anchor.snippet: A version of Bayes' theorem for 3 events

annotations.note: Iterated conditioning `cond_cond_eq_cond_inter` provides the building block, but the explicit three-event Bayes formula is not a single declaration.

annotations.label: Bayesian updating (conditionalization)

anchor.snippet: An agent who follows Bayesian updating, also known as updating by conditionalization

annotations.note: The epistemic updating rule is not formalized, though the underlying conditional probability `cond` it relies on is.
